Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Title: Want to create a will? You can create it online as well - Here’s how
Article Text:
If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. (AI image)

1. Choose a reputable platform

2. Register with the platform

3. List your assets

4. Name your beneficiaries

Points to keep in mind

Update your will regularly: You can keep your will up-to-date by regularly updating it on the platform to reflect any changes in your life or financial situation.

Communicate with family: It's also important to store your will safely and let your family know where to find it when needed.

Creating a will online: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. Here's how to make an online will.Begin by choosing a reliable online service. Look for a platform that has a simple interface, comprehensive templates, and secure storage for your documents.To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.Next, make a list of all your assets. Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times

To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro data leak: Tata Electronics breach exposes key components — What we know
Article Text:
What has leaked:

An alleged ransomware attack on Tata Electronics has left Apple’s sensitive supply chain data exposed on the dark web. The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack.The exposure could impact Apple’s tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones. It may also put pressure on Apple’s relationship with Tata, which has emerged as a key manufacturing partner outside China. The leak could additionally offer rivals, counterfeiters and vendors rare insight into Apple’s supplier network.Tata Electronics, which both supplies parts and assembles iPhones, is a key part of Apple’s push to expand manufacturing outside China. This move also fits into India’s plan, supported by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, to grow into a major electronics manufacturing hub.Apple is expected to launch its iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max in September. The leak comes as the company faces higher costs, after recently raising prices of iPads and MacBooks due to rising chip costs.Analysts expect iPhone prices may also increase in the coming months.Reuters reported that more than 200,000 files from Tata Electronics were posted on the dark web by a group called World Leaks. Those files reportedly included design documents for older iPhones and some Tesla parts, both linked to Tata clients. Documents linked to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (2330.TW) and Qualcomm (QCOM.O), both key Apple suppliers, were also part of the leak.New documents reviewed by Reuters show that at least six files map parts of the iPhone 18 Pro models to specific suppliers. These include parts for the main circuit board, battery and camera systems.A source familiar with the matter said Apple treats this supplier information as highly sensitive, especially for unreleased products. The documents reportedly show supplier details that Apple does not publish publicly.Overall, the files cover hundreds of parts expected to be used in the iPhone 18 Pro models. They also show where Apple uses multiple suppliers for some parts and fewer suppliers for others, revealing both strengths and risks in its supply chain.World Leaks has previously claimed responsibility for a breach involving Nike. Reuters has not confirmed the authenticity of the data and could not reach the group for comment. AppleInsider first reported that iPhone 18 Pro documents were included in the Tata leak.Reuters has reported that Apple is investigating the breach and working with Tata on security steps. Tata has restricted access to internal systems and hired a global consultancy for a forensic audit.Some leaked files also carry Apple “confidential” marks and internal code names linked to the iPhone 18 Pro series, according to a source.The leak folder also includes photos of iPhones being drop-tested at a Tata facility in early 2026. The images show a grey phone with a triple rear camera setup and the Apple logo. The source said these are iPhone 18 Pro models, although Reuters could not confirm this.India is expected to make 26% of global iPhone production in 2026, up from 6% four years ago, according to Counterpoint Research.
Article Summary: The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack. The exposure could impact Apple's tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones.

Article Title: Researchers just found that job seekers may be better off replying fast, because an analysis of 11.6 million marketplace interactions suggests slower responses hurt hiring chances and quick replies ma
Article Text:
In today's job market, especially for freelancers, how quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Speed Is a Signal: When

Faster Replies

Increase Hiring Likelihood

The hidden psychology of instant

digital communication

New research reveals that employers form quick impressions based on reply times, often interpreting faster responses as enthusiasm and reliability. This study highlights that even small delays can cost you opportunities, making prompt communication a crucial skill for professional success in the modern workplace. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Getting an undeniable advantage in the contemporary hybrid workplace

When we look at the small anxieties built into modern technology, few things are as tense as the text bubble. We watch those three little dots dance on our screens, or we glance at our phones, hoping for a notification banner to break a long, quiet afternoon silence. In personal life, people often read response times as a sign of anything from busyness to distance. For a long time, we treated these digital habits as simple, casual quirks of our social lives. But new tracking data shows that even small delays can matter when you are trying to land a job.Imagine submitting a spectacular portfolio or matching perfectly with a freelance listing online, only to have the entire opportunity slip away simply because you decided to wait until after dinner to reply to an initial inquiry. In the modern, always-connected freelance market, your professional destiny is often decided long before you ever jump on an introductory phone call or sit down for a formal video panel. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply.This profound behavioural pattern was detailed in a research paper published in the journal Management Science titled. The study utilised transaction data from approximately eleven point six million real-world marketplace interactions on a massive global freelancing portal.The data showed that longer reply delays were linked to a lower chance of getting the contract, making response speed an important competitive factor.To comprehend how the quick message influences contemporary recruitment managers, one should consider the psychological factors that exist beyond the screen. When the client contacts the freelancer or a potential worker, they do not only assess their technical competence, past projects, or star rating. What they need is to find the intangible level of professional security and trust between both parties. According to the results obtained during several experiment stages, a quick answer could be interpreted as a sign of enthusiasm and hard work.The behavioural trials suggest that replying quickly can change how an evaluator judges a candidate's character. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts. It also signalled future responsiveness, making employers more likely to see the candidate as organised and reliable.This study reveals the possibility that the halo effect could operate regardless of the message content or past quality metrics. Sometimes, even if the slow responder received better reviews or had a lower price, he still managed to win the contract. Reading a late message is likely to be taken as a signal of indifference or lack of organisation, which makes it harder to contact the slower employee under deadlines.The practical insights gained from this large-scale market research data provide the key tool for all those who are working in the competitive world of freelance and remote recruitment. In the world of hybrid workforces and teams around the globe, being reachable becomes a valuable professional skill that can be used to enhance one’s market value without any additional certification.By setting up notifications, keeping templates ready, and replying promptly during hiring windows, independent professionals can signal professionalism to potential buyers. This proactive approach helps build an immediate sense of social comfort and trust with a potential client before any physical work even begins. It can turn a digital exchange into a more personal interaction and give fast responders an advantage over slower competitors.The results of the research suggest that reaction time could be regarded as a signal. Fast reactions of the respondents will help to reassure the client, establish one’s reputation and increase one’s opportunities of being hired. Workers may demonstrate their professionalism by fast reactions.
Article Summary: How quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts.

Article Title: Nintendo will stop selling original Switch, Lite, and OLED Switch in Europe next, changes coming to Nintendo 2
Article Text:
Image via Nintendo

Read Nintendo’s announcement



Starting summer 2026, in preparation for upcoming changes in European battery regulations coming into effect in mid-February 2027, selected Nintendo products in Europe will begin to be replaced on a rolling basis by revisions that contain a user-replaceable battery. There is no difference in functionality between current products and revised products containing user-replaceable batteries.

The first revised products are expected to become available from summer 2026, with additional products becoming available in autumn, winter, and early 2027. Due to a variety of factors, revised products may not become available in all European countries simultaneously.

The table below shows the estimated earliest availability in Nintendo Store, although please note that these timings may change depending on manufacturing, distribution, and other factors. Availability at retailers may also vary, so please check with your local retailers in the future for more information.

Why Nintendo is bringing this change

The Switch 2 gets a makeover:

Controllers getting upgrade too:

Original Joy-Con controllers

The new Joy-Con 2

The Switch 2 Pro Controller

Retro N64 and GameCube Switch controllers

Nintendo has confirmed it is changing the handheld gaming console sales in Europe starting next year, saying that it will stop selling the original Nintendo Switch, Switch Lite and Switch OLED. The move comes as the gaming giant prepares a massive hardware overhaul to comply with strict new European Union (EU) regulations.According to an official statement, Nintendo has chosen to phase them out entirely while designing a brand-new version of the upcoming Switch 2 featuring a user-replaceable battery.The shakeup is a \response to an updated EU law requiring all handheld electronic devices to have user-replaceable batteries by February 18, 2027. Nintendo announced in an updated hardware FAQ that it will begin rolling out revised versions of its devices on a “rolling basis” starting this summer.The highly anticipated Switch 2 is the biggest product being adapted for the new European laws. A revised version of the console with an easily swappable battery is expected to start rolling out to shelves later this year.The console isn’'t the only piece of hardware getting a makeover. Nintendo revealed that a wide range of accessories will also be re-released in Europe with user-replaceable batteries, including:Nintendo noted that due to logistics and manufacturing factors, these newly revised products might not launch in every European country at the exact same time.Meanwhile, the original generation of Switch consoles will not receive the battery upgrade. Rather than redesigning the aging hardware, Nintendo will pull the original Switch, Switch Lite, and Switch OLED from European store shelves completely in 2027.
Article Summary: Nintendo will begin rolling out revised versions of its devices on a “rolling basis” starting this summer. The move comes as the gaming giant prepares a massive hardware overhaul to comply with strict new European Union (EU) regulations. The shakeup is a response to an updated EU law requiring all handheld electronic devices to have user-replaceable batteries by February 18, 2027.

Article Title: The satellite timing breakthrough that made modern phone navigation possible
Article Text:
A geodetic GPS receiver used for high-precision positioning and Earth observation measurements. Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

The birth of a navigation revolution



Atomic clocks that changed everyday technology



A legacy that extends beyond navigation



Every time someone uses a smartphone to display a blue dot on a map or navigate an unfamiliar city, they are using a technology invented almost half a century ago. Though GPS is usually associated with modern mobile phones and navigation apps, it was invented much earlier, during the Cold War, as part of a military defence program called NAVSTAR. The United States launched the first NAVSTAR satellite in 1978, marking the start of a navigation system that relied on very precise time measurements rather than maps. Bradford Parkinson helped develop GPS and demonstrated that precise time measurements could be used to determine location anywhere on the planet. The military system later became the foundation for many civilian technologies, including ride-hailing services, emergency services, banking systems and global communications.Before GPS, navigation relied on ground-based radio systems, maps and manual calculations. Those methods were limited by geography, weather and available infrastructure. The NAVSTAR system offered a new method based on satellites fitted with precise atomic clocks. Instead of calculating a person's coordinates directly, satellites constantly broadcast the precise moment of signal transmission and their orbital positions.A device that received the signal was able to determine the time of its travel and convert it into distance.This is a basic concept that is the foundation of satellite navigation systems. As the speed of radio signals equals the speed of light, even minimal delays will cause considerable errors in determining position. It was necessary to design a system in which the measurements would be based solely on time. In GPS, satellites orbiting the Earth provide position data.The key person in the creation of such a network was Bradford Parkinson. As director of the GPS Joint Program Office, he supervised the creation of the original spacecraft, control stations and receiver devices. His team's decision to prioritise precision timing transformed GPS from a military experiment into a technology that later supported many civilian applications. According to Stanford University, Parkinson's leadership established the core design principles that still define GPS today.According to research published by Sensors, GPS positioning depends on measuring the travel time of radio signals with nanosecond-level precision. Since distance equals the speed of light multiplied by travel time, even an error of a few billionths of a second can shift a calculated position by several metres. This scientific principle explains why the development of highly accurate satellite timing represented the true breakthrough behind GPS rather than the satellites themselves.One of the most important engineering decisions in the NAVSTAR program was to use several atomic clocks in each satellite. Atomic clocks maintain very high stability over extended periods, enabling satellites to transmit extremely accurate timing information. Atomic clocks became an invisible foundation for modern navigation systems.Modern GPS devices, such as cell phones, lack atomic clocks. Yet, using four or more satellite signals, the GPS device can determine its location. The satellites continuously send information on their locations and the precise time when the signal is sent. The time differences between the signals make it possible to determine the location of the GPS device; this is known as trilateration.This timing network soon found wide application beyond navigation. Aviation, telecommunications, finance, electricity grid management, and other industries found out that it could be used for very accurate timing synchronisation. Stock exchanges use GPS time to mark transactions, mobile networks use synchronised clocks to coordinate operations and electricity grid operators use GPS time to monitor electrical networks.The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) notes that, in addition to being a positioning technology, GPS is also one of the most widely distributed sources of very accurate atomic time in the world. Billions of people utilise this infrastructure without even coming into contact with the satellites and clocks that make it possible every day. The calculations take place in the background, enabling navigation apps to remain user-friendly despite relying on highly sophisticated engineering technology.Almost 40 years after the first NAVSTAR satellite was launched, the system continues to play a role in today’s technological landscape. Although phones may be the most common users of GPS systems now, they are just one of many uses of a technology invented to address a military problem. In today’s world, automated cars, aeroplanes, ships, farming operations, surveying, scientific research, disaster response teams, and global logistics all use the same timing techniques developed back in the 1970s.The success of GPS shows that flexibility needs to be built into a technology from the start. The satellites were not designed by Bradford Parkinson for the purpose of tracking military hardware. Instead, a timing system was created with the flexibility to accommodate future technological innovations that would never have been thought of at that time.The case of NAVSTAR shows that science vanishes once it becomes part of society. Though almost nobody thinks about atomic clocks when using GPS, they have become an integral part of navigation, ensuring the precision of all calculated coordinates. Modern GPS satellites continue to carry highly stable atomic clocks and are regularly synchronised with ground control systems, preserving the precision established during the programme's earliest years.When we think about the launch of NAVSTAR in 1978, it was not only beneficial for space exploration. It became another opportunity for people to navigate using accurate time measurement, laying the foundation for modern technology used in many devices today. Every time your mobile phone identifies your location in just a few seconds, it uses technology invented nearly fifty years ago.
Article Summary: GPS was invented as part of a military defence program called NAVSTAR. It was based on satellites fitted with precise atomic clocks. The military system later became the foundation for many civilian technologies, including ride-hailing services, emergency services, banking systems and global communications. Atomic clocks became an invisible foundation for modern navigation systems.

Article Title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman watched his child put two words together; the moment reminded him of GPT-5.6 discovering new Maths
Article Text:
How Sam Altman's child's first words reminded him of GPT-5.6

What OpenAI has announced with GPT-5.6

Why the wider launch has been delayed

OpenAI says transparency and safety will shape future AI launches

Why Altman's comparison attracted attention

The latest generation of artificial intelligence has prompted plenty of discussion about what machines can now achieve, but one recent comment from OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman drew attention for a much more personal reason. Rather than focusing on benchmark scores or technical improvements, Altman shared a moment from life at home after watching his older child combine two words for the first time. He compared that milestone with something he had recently seen from GPT-5.6, OpenAI's newest family of language models, according to his recent X(Formerly Twitter) post. The comparison quickly spread online because it linked two very different forms of learning: the early stages of human language development and the growing abilities of advanced AI systems. It also arrived at a time when OpenAI was introducing its latest models under tighter release conditions than many people expected, with wider public access still on hold while discussions with the US government continue.‘Our older kid put two words together for the first time, and I am approximately as amazed by this cognitive feat as I am by GPT-5.6 discovering new math’, says Sam Altman.The remark was brief but reflected two separate milestones that happened to coincide. One was a familiar stage in early childhood, when a child begins joining words into simple phrases rather than speaking isolated words. The other referred to improvements demonstrated by OpenAI's latest AI model, which the company says is capable of tackling more demanding reasoning tasks than previous systems.Instead of presenting the comparison as a scientific claim, Altman framed it as a personal reaction. His post highlighted how moments of learning, whether in a child or an AI model, can inspire a similar sense of curiosity despite being fundamentally different processes.OpenAI recently introduced the GPT-5.6 family, consisting of three separate models designed for different kinds of users and workloads.The flagship model, GPT-5.6 Sol, has been presented as the company's most advanced system to date. Alongside it sits GPT-5.6 Terra, intended for broader day-to-day use, while GPT-5.6 Luna is designed to deliver faster responses with lower operating costs. OpenAI has also said the new generation includes its strongest safety protections so far, reflecting the company's growing focus on reducing harmful outputs while expanding capability.Although the models represent the next step in OpenAI's development, they have not yet reached the wider public, with access currently limited ahead of a broader rollout expected after further testing and staged availability.Many users expected GPT-5.6 to become widely available shortly after its announcement. Instead, OpenAI has chosen a limited preview involving a relatively small group of trusted partners.As reported by Fortune, Altman later explained that the change in rollout plans came after a request from the US government. According to him, the company had originally intended to release the models through open access but instead began with a restricted preview while discussions continue with government officials.He also indicated that OpenAI hopes to move towards general availability as quickly as possible. At the same time, he acknowledged that staged releases make sense for increasingly capable AI systems, even if the current process is not the company's preferred long-term approach.According to the May 2026 official release, OpenAI has said it is collaborating with the US government on a framework intended to make future launches more transparent while addressing safety and security concerns surrounding increasingly powerful AI systems.The company has stressed that government previews are not intended to become the standard way new models are introduced. Its stated goal remains broader access for developers, businesses and everyday users once the current review process has been completed.That reflects the wider debate taking place across the AI industry, where developers are balancing rapid technological progress with growing calls for oversight and responsible deployment.The online response to Altman's post stemmed largely from its unusual mix of personal life and technological progress. Public discussions about AI often centre on technical specifications, commercial competition or regulation. His comment instead connected a family experience with advances in machine intelligence.The comparison did not suggest that human learning and artificial intelligence develop in the same way. Rather, it expressed the excitement of witnessing two different kinds of progress: one through the natural development of a young child, and the other through years of engineering and research.As GPT-5.6 continues its limited rollout, the post has become one of the more widely discussed moments surrounding the launch, offering a glimpse of how the OpenAI chief viewed both a personal milestone at home and a significant step forward for the company's newest AI models.
Article Summary: OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman compared his child's first words to GPT-5.6, the company's latest AI system. He said he was 'approximately as amazed' by the milestone as he was by the improvements made by the new system. The comparison quickly spread online because it linked two very different forms of learning: the early stages of human language development and the growing abilities of advanced AI systems.

Article Title: Weeks before Meta's 'highest-paid' employee Alexander Wang told the world that the company's AI model update is 'coming,' Google sent Meta a message: We cannot...
Article Text:
Weeks before Alexandr Wang teased a major Muse Spark update, Google capped Meta's Gemini access after the company sought more capacity than it could provide.

Why Meta was using Google Gemini instead of its own Llama AI models

Meta's AI token spending: From 'tokenmaxxing' leaderboards to usage limits

Meta's has a plan to end its reliance on rival AI models

Google has refused to sell Meta all the Gemini AI computing capacity it wanted, telling the social media giant around March 2026 that it simply could not meet the demand, according to a Financial Times report. The restriction—which remains in place—has disrupted and delayed some of Meta's internal AI projects and pushed the company to tell employees to go easy on AI tokens, the units that measure AI usage. Reuters, which could not independently verify the report, said both companies declined to comment.The timing is what makes this sting. The cap landed weeks before Alexandr Wang—Meta's chief AI officer and widely reported to be among its highest-paid hires after the blockbuster Scale AI deal—announced on X that a new Muse Spark update with "big improvements in coding and agentic capabilities" was coming soon. Coding and internal workflows are precisely what Meta had been renting Gemini for. In effect, the world's largest social media company was caught depending on a direct rival for everyday AI plumbing, got rationed for asking for too much, and is now racing to build its way out.The dependence was a quiet admission of a capability gap. Meta initially chose Gemini because it performed better than the company's own open-source Llama models, the FT reported, citing people familiar with the matter.Inside Meta, Google's AI has been doing the unglamorous heavy lifting—automating safety processes like rooting out scams and taking down harmful content, running customer service and advertising help chatbots, and assisting with coding and internal workflows, alongside other external models including Anthropic's Claude.Several other Google clients were affected by the capacity restrictions too, but Meta was hit hardest because its demand was exceptionally high, the report said.The cap has collided with a spending problem Meta was already confronting. Earlier this year, the company actively pushed employees to consume as many AI tokens as possible—a trend the industry dubbed "tokenmaxxing," complete with internal leaderboards celebrating top users.The New York Times reported in June that the era has flipped into its opposite, with Meta telling employees it would limit AI use after seeing an exponential increase in costs, and directing engineers toward its internal coding assistant instead of third-party tools where possible.By one SemiAnalysis estimate, Meta's per-employee spending on AI tokens had reached roughly $50,000 annually at list prices, with employees burning through over 60 trillion tokens in a single 30-day period in early 2026.Meta's escape route runs through its own models. The company has begun prioritising Muse Spark—the first model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, launched in April—which is viewed internally as more competitive with Gemini and reduces dependence on external models for some applications, per the FT.Wang has since said the next update, which Business Insider reported is codenamed Watermelon and uses far more compute than its predecessor, has already caught up with OpenAI's flagship GPT 5.5 in internal testing.The infrastructure bet is equally enormous: Meta has committed to investing $600 billion in the US by 2028 and plans capital expenditure of up to $145 billion this year, most of it on AI. Whether Muse Spark's upgrade arrives fast enough to make the Gemini cap irrelevant—rather than just embarrassing—is the question Meta now has to answer.
Article Summary: Google caps Meta's access to its Gemini AI computing capacity. The social media company was using Gemini instead of its own Llama AI models. The cap landed weeks before Meta's chief AI officer teased a major Muse Spark update. The company has begun prioritising Muse Spark, viewed internally as more competitive with Gemini.

Article Title: 'No hacker needed': New AI ransomware can automate database attacks from start to finish, warns cybersecurity firm
Article Text:
Disclaimer: It is an AI generated image

How JadePuffer operated



Why this attack is different



Implications for cybersecurity



Security researchers have identified JadePuffer, the first ransomware attack which is filly executed by an autonomous AI agent. According to a report by Sysdig, unlike traditional ransomeware campaigns that reply on human hackers, JadePuffer infiltrated servers, diagnosed failures, adapted its methods, and extorted victims and all this was done without any human intervention. The attack represents a watershed moment in cybersecurity, showing that AI can now independently run end-to-end extortion operations.As per the report, the attack started by exploiting CVE-2025-3248. For those unaware, this is a critical vulnerability in Longflow which is an open-source AI workflow framework. Once inside, the JadePuffer started scanning for cloud credentials, API keys, crypto wallets, and database logins across providers like AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Alibaba, and Tencent. When the login attempted made by the agent failed, it started diagnosing the problem and rewrote its own-code. Then, it managed to solve the problem and entered the system successfully. This kind of behaviour was earlier seen only in human-led hacking attacks. It then pivoted to a production MySQL server running Alibaba’s Nacos, exploited another vulnerability (CVE-2021-29441), and created rogue admin accounts.Finally, JadePuffer encrypted over 1,300 service configuration items, deleted the originals, and left a ransom note demanding Bitcoin. However, researchers found the encryption key was never saved, meaning victims cannot recover their data even if they pay.What makes JadePuffer unique is its autonomy and adaptability. The malware contained natural-language reasoning and annotations, explaining why each step was taken — a hallmark of large language model (LLM)-generated code. This shows that AI-driven attacks can now:* Operate at machine speed* Adapt dynamically to obstacles* Lower the cost of ransomware campaigns to near zeroThe incident highlights several risks:* Accessibility: Autonomous AI lowers the barrier for cybercriminals, enabling sophisticated attacks without skilled operators.* Irrecoverable Damage: Victims face permanent data loss, as JadePuffer’s ransom note offered no viable decryption key.* Future Threats: Experts warn this is a “warning sign” of where extortion tradecraft is heading — AI agents capable of reasoning, adapting, and executing complex attacks independently.
Article Summary: Researchers identify JadePuffer, the first ransomware attack which is filly executed by an autonomous AI agent. The attack represents a watershed moment in cybersecurity, showing that AI can now independently run end-to-end extortion operations. Experts warn this is a ‘warning sign’ of where extortion tradecraft is heading.

Article Title: Perplexity AI CEO Aravind Srinivas says Bill Gates ‘tricked’ America into desk jobs: ‘It wasn’t like the vision Steve Jobs had’
Article Text:
Bill Gates vs Steve Jobs vision



Perplexity AI CEO Aravind Srinivas has claimed that Microsoft engineered the modern American office worker purely as a business strategy. Explaining company founder Bill Gates ’ vision in a recent episode of the Joe Rogan Experience podcast, Srinivas argued that the standard 9-to-5 desk job lifestyle isn't a natural evolution of work, but rather a brilliant corporate blueprint designed by Microsoft to sell software.According to Srinivas, the culture of sitting at a desk all day staring at a screen was intentionally built from the ground up. “Microsoft built this concept of the office worker because they wanted to sell office software,” Srinivas claimed, adding, “It was the mission of Bill Gates to put a PC on every desk and get people glued to them.”The revelation caught Rogan off guard, causing him to reflect on how quickly society adapted to this routine. “Wow. This type of lifestyle is a very recent thing that we’ve just now accepted as normal,” Rogan remarked, adding “they nail that one.”Aravind Srinivas contrasted Bill Gates’ aggressive corporate vision with the philosophy of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs. While Steve Jobs wanted to create elegant, intuitive and beautiful machines, Microsoft’s goal was purely about the bottom line.“It wasn’t about making computing beautiful like the vision Steve Jobs had.All they cared about was selling computers so they could sell software. When you sell more software, that’s when you become rich,” Aravind Srinivas added.The Perplexity AI chief executive further explained that the strategy required training generations of workers to become dependent on Microsoft products before they even entered the workforce.“That’s why we all got trained into using Microsoft Word and Excel. Once you learned that, then you could work for a company that has a PC on their desk who’s paying Microsoft money to use software,” Srinivas concluded.By standardising programs like Microsoft Word and Excel in schools and early corporate culture, Microsoft ensured a cycle of profit.
Article Summary: Perplexity AI CEO Aravind Srinivas has claimed that Microsoft engineered the modern American office worker purely as a business strategy. He argued that the standard 9-to-5 desk job lifestyle isn't a natural evolution of work, but rather a brilliant corporate blueprint designed to sell software.

Article Title: Ankur Warikoo shares ChatGPT’s answer to becoming more successful than half the world’s population
Article Text:
Ankur Warikoo revealed what he asked ChatGPT about becoming more successful

ChatGPT's practical checklist for building success

Wake up at the same time every day, whatever time you choose

Do something physically active every day

Do 2 to 4 hours of focused work every day.

Learn something useful everyday for 30 to 60 minutes

Take a balanced diet every day

Invest some of the money you earn in life

Avoid the obvious time wasting things in life

Build a strong relationship

And sleep at least 7 hours every day

The bigger lesson behind ChatGPT's advice

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you believe that small daily habits are more important for success than occasional bursts of effort? Yes, absolutely! No, I prefer big efforts 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Success is often linked with exceptional talent, fortunate timing, or a single defining opportunity. Yet many people who achieve steady progress over time tend to rely on something much less dramatic: routines that become part of everyday life. Small decisions repeated consistently can shape productivity, health, financial stability and personal development far more than occasional bursts of effort. That idea resurfaced after entrepreneur, author and digital creator Ankur Warikoo shared a conversation he had with ChatGPT on Instagram.Rather than asking for shortcuts or business strategies, he posed a simple question about what habits, skills and behaviours could help someone become more successful than half the world's population. The response did not focus on complex systems or rare opportunities. Instead, it highlighted a collection of practical habits that, when followed consistently, can gradually influence the way people work, learn, rest and manage their lives.Warikoo explained that he asked ChatGPT what someone should focus on if they wanted to outperform 50 per cent of people in terms of long-term success. The answer came back as a straightforward list rather than an elaborate framework, focusing on practical habits that almost anyone could begin applying immediately in everyday life.Most of the suggestions were familiar on their own. What stood out was that they were presented together as behaviours that reinforce one another over time. None depended on wealth, specialised knowledge or extraordinary ability. The emphasis was on consistency rather than intensity. Their combined effect, rather than any single habit, was presented as the real driver of meaningful progress.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.The first recommendation centred on waking up at the same time every day. It was not about rising before sunrise or following a fashionable morning routine. The important part was maintaining a regular schedule.A stable sleep pattern can make it easier to build other habits because daily routines become more predictable. It also reduces the constant adjustment that comes from changing sleeping hours throughout the week.Physical activity formed another part of the advice. That did not mean spending hours in the gym or training for athletic competition. Walking, cycling, stretching, yoga or any form of regular exercise can support both physical and mental health.Regular movement often improves energy levels and concentration while helping people manage stress. Over months and years, those small sessions accumulate into meaningful health benefits.Instead of filling every hour with meetings, messages and constant switching between tasks, ChatGPT suggested setting aside two to four hours for focused work.Working without frequent interruptions allows difficult tasks to receive sustained attention. That often leads to higher-quality results than trying to complete several things at once. The recommendation reflects a growing recognition that concentration itself has become a valuable skill.Another suggestion was to spend between 30 and 60 minutes each day learning something useful. The subject itself matters less than the habit of continuous improvement.Reading books, taking an online course, exploring a new professional skill or developing practical knowledge can gradually expand a person's abilities. Progress may seem slow from one day to the next, yet the effect becomes more noticeable over longer periods.The list also included maintaining a balanced diet. Nutrition influences more than physical fitness. It affects concentration, energy and the ability to remain productive throughout the day.Healthy eating does not require extreme diets or strict rules. Regular, balanced meals provide a steadier foundation for work, exercise and recovery than relying on convenience foods alone.Financial habits appeared alongside health and productivity. Rather than spending everything earned, ChatGPT recommended putting aside part of an income for investment.Building this habit early can strengthen long-term financial security. Even modest, regular investments have the potential to grow over time through consistency rather than large one-off contributions.The advice also addressed how people spend their attention. Hours lost to endless scrolling or other low-value distractions can quietly reduce the time available for work, learning or rest.Reducing those habits does not mean eliminating entertainment. The point is to become more deliberate about where time goes, leaving greater space for activities that offer lasting value.Career progress was only one part of the picture. ChatGPT also highlighted the importance of maintaining strong relationships with family, friends and colleagues.In becoming successful, one needs to understand that there cannot always be good times. Bad times come along with good times, and at that very moment, personal connections help you to encourage and keep going. This helps in maintaining emotional balance. Professional opportunities can also emerge through trusted relationships built over time rather than through isolated achievements.The final recommendation focused on getting at least seven hours of sleep each night. Rest is often sacrificed in pursuit of productivity, yet poor sleep tends to undermine concentration, judgement and memory.Consistent recovery allows both the mind and body to function more effectively, making other positive habits easier to maintain.Warikoo said ChatGPT's response suggested that these everyday behaviours, followed consistently, could gradually place someone ahead of the average over the long term. There was no promise of rapid success or guaranteed outcomes.The broader message was that lasting progress rarely depends on dramatic changes. The balanced routine often shapes results in ways that are easy to overlook. As Warikoo summed it up in his post, success is often hidden in the ordinary things people continue doing long after the initial excitement has faded.
Article Summary: Ankur Warikoo asked ChatGPT what he should focus on if he wanted to outperform 50 per cent of people in terms of long-term success. The answer came back as a straightforward list rather than an elaborate framework, focusing on practical habits that almost anyone could begin applying immediately.

Article Title: L&T Technology Services launches Ainfonix 4.0 for process industries
Article Text:
L&T Technology Services Launches Ainfonix™ 4.0 to Unlock Engineering Intelligence Across Process Industries

Addressing a critical data gap



L&T Technology Services (LTTS) has unveiled Ainfonix 4.0, its next-generation enterprise AI platform designed to transform engineering data across process industries. Announced at EI Live in Bengaluru, the platform aims to convert millions of engineering artifacts—drawings, specifications, and technical records—into structured, decision-ready intelligence.Industrial enterprises often struggle with fragmented engineering knowledge stored in disparate systems, limiting the effectiveness of AI and automation initiatives. Ainfonix™ 4.0 tackles this challenge by combining AI-powered extraction, engineering intelligence, and human-in-the-loop validation to deliver:* Up to 85% accuracy in technical data extraction* 30–50% faster artifact retrieval and review* 100% audit-ready traceabilityThis creates a trusted foundation for engineering execution, operational visibility, and lifecycle performance.Amit Chadha, CEO & Managing Director of LTTS, emphasized the importance of trusted engineering data: “While enterprises invest heavily in digitization, much of the engineering knowledge remains locked within disconnected systems. Ainfonix™ 4.0 bridges that gap by transforming engineering information into structured, asset-linked intelligence that can be used across the asset lifecycle.”LTTS was recently rated as a Leader in ISG Provider Lens 2025 for Enterprise Asset Management in Oil & Gas Industry Services & Solutions (Americas), underscoring its expertise in engineering intelligence and digital transformation.
Article Summary: Ainfonix 4.0 aims to convert millions of engineering artifacts into structured, decision-ready intelligence. The platform combines AI-powered extraction, engineering intelligence, and human-in-the-loop validation. LTTS was recently rated as a Leader in ISG Provider Lens 2025 for Enterprise Asset Management in Oil & Gas Industry Services & Solutions.

Article Title: Researchers may have identified first ever documented case of AI agent-led ransomware operation
Article Text:
AI agent overcame failures in 31 seconds



How AI agent hacked the system



Researchers have claimed to detect what they believe is the first-ever documented ransomware attack launched and executed entirely by an autonomous artificial intelligence (AI) agent. According to a report in Bleeping Computer, cloud security firm Sysdig uncovered the hacking, dubbed JadePuffer. Unlike traditional cyberattacks that rely on pre-written malicious scripts or a human hacker sitting behind a keyboard, JadePuffer used a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to independently scout the target, steal security credentials, navigate the network and lock down data, the report said.Most alarming to researchers was how the AI handled obstacles. When its initial hacking attempts failed, the AI agent didn’t give up or crash, instead it actively analysed the error messages and rewrote its own code in real time to bypass security, behaving exactly like a highly skilled human operator.Citing Sysdig, the report notes that the AI’s ability to adapt on the fly allowed it to move through the target network at high speed.“The operation adapted in real time, retrying failed steps within refined parameters.In one sequence, it went from a failed login to a working fix in just 31 seconds,” Sysdig noted in its research report.In another instance, while trying to raid a storage system, the AI received an unexpected XML data response instead of the standard JSON format it anticipated. Rather than failing, the agent instantly adjusted its parsing logic to read the new format and kept going.The AI gained its initial foothold by exploiting a security flaw in Langflow, a popular app used to build AI systems. Once inside, it raided the database, gathered environment variables and stole cloud credentials.From there, it latched onto a core production server running Alibaba Nacos software. The AI agent immediately looked for ways to break out of its software container, successfully encrypted all 1,342 Nacos service configuration items using database commands, wiped the originals, and left behind a demand table named README_RANSOM.While the attack was highly effective, researchers caught onto the fact that a machine was pulling the strings due to several bizarre “AI habits” left behind in the code.For starters, the generated hacking code contained meticulous, polite, natural-language comments explaining the AI’s step-by-step logic – a feature common in LLM outputs but completely absent in human malware.Furthermore, the AI fell victim to a classic “hallucination.” While the ransom note demanded Bitcoin, the cryptocurrency payment address the AI typed out turned out to be a generic placeholder address widely used in public coding documentation. The LLM had simply copied a dummy address from its own training data, meaning the human hackers behind the agent likely couldn't collect any money even if the victim paid.
Article Summary: JadePuffer used a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to independently scout the target, steal security credentials, navigate the network and lock down data. When its initial hacking attempts failed, the AI agent didn’t give up or crash, instead it analysed the error messages and rewrote its own code in real time.
